purpose: Complex Runtime Kit capability candidates for project-level continuous governance. This file is a discovery aid, not proof that a skill, tool, plugin, connector, or API is callable in the current environment.

capabilities.name: complex_runtime_repo_skill
capabilities.use_when: A Codex agent is asked to use Complex, design a Complex prompt, run continuous cadence, align Plan or Goal mode, or govern a long-running project.
capabilities.boundary: The official repo-scoped workflow entry is .agents/skills/complex-runtime/SKILL.md. The skill summarizes workflow; protocol/core.md remains authoritative.
capabilities.verification_hint: Confirm the response mentions codex_surface_alignment, thread_goal or phase_goal, beat_objective, responsibility boundary, and next_route before business execution.

capabilities.name: portfolio_control_plane_orchestration
capabilities.use_when: A continuous project has multiple modules, standing lanes, sources, deliverables, or repeated guardrail-only beats.
capabilities.boundary: This is a manager-thread operating model, not a platform thread or automation. It should guide beat routing and platform-resource fit; actual resource creation follows current Codex surface/tool semantics.
capabilities.verification_hint: Record target_function, module_portfolio, standing_lane_portfolio, forward_indexes, branch_parking, Hot/Warm/Cold state, and forward_artifact_acceptance before local greedy execution.

capabilities.name: operating_organization_multi_lane
capabilities.use_when: A long project needs controller, expert, review, literature/data, code, writing, or human-interface responsibilities to recur across beats.
capabilities.boundary: A lane is a responsibility, not automatically a user-visible thread. AI decides whether platform resources fit; actual creation follows current Codex surface/tool semantics.
capabilities.verification_hint: Record controller, human_interface, literature_data_acquisition, model_component, data_code, review_risk, and writing_delivery lane contracts plus context-reset rules.

capabilities.name: external_calibration_and_hallucination_sentinel
capabilities.use_when: A strategic route, project operating model, model choice, evaluation rule, protocol default, or public claim is being changed or relied on for many future beats.
capabilities.boundary: Use official docs, primary papers, standards, or mature production writeups. Routine beats may reuse a fresh calibration note instead of browsing again.
capabilities.verification_hint: Record source, problem_matched, adopted, rejected, not_transferable, Complex_micro_contract, refresh_trigger, unsupported claims, and falsification cues.

capabilities.name: trace_appraisal_and_state_lightening
capabilities.use_when: A continuous project has many beats, large state files, historical artifacts, old gates, or context-pollution risk.
capabilities.boundary: Do not delete audit records by default. Demote stale material from Hot State to Warm Index or Cold Archive with recoverable pointers.
capabilities.verification_hint: Record trace_appraisal_status, retention class, hot_state_location, warm_index_locations, cold_archive_policy, and demoted pointers.

capabilities.name: attention_governance_minimum_closure
capabilities.use_when: A research, analysis, prototype, or long-running project risks spending many beats on local audits, source gathering, or reporting before showing an end-to-end chain.
capabilities.boundary: Minimum closure is a judgment signal, not final proof. Minimum observability must not hide evidence gaps, unsupported claims, or responsibility boundaries.
capabilities.verification_hint: Record minimum_viable_closure_status, closure_chain, minimum_sufficient_observability_status, last_progress_signal, and heavy_audit_trigger.

capabilities.name: complex_protocol_onboarding
capabilities.use_when: A new project asks to use Complex, Auto Research, or this repository as a governing method.
capabilities.boundary: Read the relevant protocol components first, then bind only the parts that fit the current project.
capabilities.verification_hint: Confirm the project has a state, evidence, decision, loop, and delivery skeleton before business execution starts.

capabilities.name: protocol_scan_sequence
capabilities.use_when: The user asks to fully scan Complex or use Complex as the governing method in another project.
capabilities.boundary: Scan order must be explicit; do not infer the protocol from a few familiar keywords.
capabilities.verification_hint: Record protocol_scan_order, keyword_trigger_map, adopt_now, skip_now, backlog, and manual_action.

capabilities.name: capability_discovery_cadence
capabilities.use_when: Starting a task, changing phase, hitting a blocker, approaching an external write, crossing a subagent boundary, or making a final claim.
capabilities.boundary: Do not force tool usage; record selected, rejected, backlog, and manual-action candidates.
capabilities.verification_hint: Check that named skills, tools, APIs, accounts, browsers, or databases were explicitly considered.

capabilities.name: scheduled_topology_capability_review
capabilities.use_when: A project is running in continuous cadence, standing lanes, user-visible threads, automations, subagents, or persistent external tools.
capabilities.boundary: Use event triggers first. Three beats is only a fallback cap; no event means lightweight_keep.
capabilities.verification_hint: Record beat_index, topology_refresh_due, capability_refresh_due, adjust_or_keep decisions, and next review trigger.

capabilities.name: local_file_search
capabilities.use_when: Repository facts, protocol anchors, templates, examples, or validation scripts can answer the question locally.
capabilities.boundary: Prefer local truth before asking the user or relying on memory.
capabilities.verification_hint: Use focused searches such as rg, rg --files, sed, find, or repository-specific scripts.

capabilities.name: complex_integrity_verifier
capabilities.use_when: Protocol, current-state, quickstart, behavior pack, Runtime Kit templates, or examples are edited.
capabilities.boundary: This verifies active repository structure; it does not prove a human-facing deliverable is good.

capabilities.name: runtime_templates
capabilities.use_when: A project needs lightweight records for state, evidence, decisions, search, questions, loop checks, or delivery contracts.
capabilities.boundary: Templates are optional landing pads, not new mandatory protocol fields.
capabilities.verification_hint: Confirm the project can resume from state.md and can justify claims through evidence.md and decision.md.

capabilities.name: browser_or_institutional_access
capabilities.use_when: Public search is insufficient and the user names browser access, Scholar, database access, institutional accounts, or uploaded materials.
capabilities.boundary: The agent must not handle passwords or bypass authorization; user login and sensitive account actions stay manual.
capabilities.verification_hint: Record what page or source the user should open and what evidence should be returned after access.

capabilities.name: subagent_or_thread_coordination
capabilities.use_when: Standing lanes, temporary subagents, user-visible threads, worktrees, automations, or clean-context review would reduce risk or latency.
capabilities.boundary: Do not equate subagents with threads or standing lanes. Probe capability, respect tool semantics, and define contracts before delegation or platform-resource creation.
capabilities.verification_hint: Record topology choice, fact ledger or context packet, output contract, integration responsibility, observability evidence, and cleanup or stop condition.

capabilities.name: goal_lifecycle_alignment
capabilities.use_when: Continuous cadence, Goal mode, versioned work, or loop engineering could create stale goals.
capabilities.boundary: Use Codex Goal as thread_goal or phase_goal. Keep current beat details in beat_objective and recovery continuity in goal_memory_summary and next_route.
capabilities.verification_hint: Compare thread_goal, phase_goal, goal_memory_summary, current_basis, next_route, project version, and delivery object before each beat.

capabilities.name: document_or_render_qa
capabilities.use_when: The final artifact is a Word document, PDF, slide deck, image, website, or other rendered deliverable.
capabilities.boundary: Rendering tools vary by environment; do not claim visual QA without fresh evidence.
capabilities.verification_hint: Check source/render freshness, output path, page or screenshot evidence, and residual warnings.
